plan_adherence_notes: All 5 planned steps executed faithfully. Step 1 planned 3 inference adapter error tests, execution added 4 (including InferenceUnsupportedQueryError — a reasonable addition). Step 2 planned 1 fallback test, execution added 2 (also testing non-500 re-raise — a valuable complement). Steps 3-4 matched exactly. Step 5 confirmed: 333 tests pass (325 existing + 8 new). Estimated ~210 lines, actual ~206 lines. Only test files were modified as scoped. Slight deviation: plan suggested adding to existing classes where appropriate, execution created 4 new focused classes — arguably cleaner organization.
research_quality_notes: Research correctly identified and corrected two wrong assumptions (a3, a5): analytics (7 existing tests) and resolve (10+ existing tests) were NOT the primary gaps. The corrected targets — 500 error handling (zero tests existed), PnL double-404 edge case, and analytics malformed timestamps — were well-supported with specific line references in server.py. WebSocket exclusion was well-reasoned with technical justification (RFC 6455 framing, async coordination). All 3 questions answered with confidence >= 0.80.
missed_questions: Should tests verify HTTP-layer 500 responses (via HTTPConnection) in addition to route_request-level errors? The plan chose route_request only, which doesn't test the do_POST/do_GET exception handling wrappers.
Are there any known flaky or slow tests in the existing suite that might interfere with CI? The 333-test suite runs in ~6.5s which is fine, but this wasn't assessed upfront.
Should the new test classes follow any specific naming convention or be grouped with existing classes for discoverability?
reason: Execution faithfully followed the plan with minor beneficial additions (extra test for InferenceUnsupportedQueryError, non-500 re-raise test). Research was thorough — correctly identified and corrected initial assumptions to target the real coverage gaps. All 333 tests pass. No production code was modified. The 8 new tests cover 4 previously untested error paths: inference adapter 500s (compile, query_marginals, query_atomic, unsupported_query), probability-edit fallback on inference failure, PnL double-404 edge case, and analytics malformed-timestamp handling.
